data.title: Opinion: Miner migration exacerbates bearish sentiment; Bitcoin may fall below $60,000.
data.description: Bitcoin miners are showing signs of migration due to rising electricity costs and declining hashrate. Data shows that the average electricity cost to mine a single Bitcoin is currently around $59,450, with net production costs around $74,300. Although Bitcoin is trading at around $82,500, higher than the average miner cost, Charles Edwards, founder of Capriole Investments, says the market still has room to fall to the $74,300 to $59,450 range. Furthermore, Bitcoin's hashrate dropped to a mid-2025 level at the end of January, which analysts believe is related to miners reallocating resources to AI projects and winter storms in the US. According to energy value models, Bitcoin's current fair value is around $120,950. Jeff Feng, co-founder of Sei Labs, stated that network difficulty adjusts after a hashrate decline, and historically, Bitcoin has typically reverted to its energy value after a prolonged decline. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitcoin's Energy Costs Signal Potential Price Decline Amid Miner Exodus
data.description:
Bitcoin's electricity cost metric is raising concerns for bullish investors, with a potential 'miner exodus' contributing to a bearish outlook. According to Cointelegraph, the cryptocurrency could see its value drop toward the $59,000–$74,000 range, which aligns with the estimated costs of mining a single Bitcoin. As of January, the average electricity cost to mine one Bitcoin is approximately $59,450, while the net production expenditure stands at about $74,300, based on data from Capriole Investments, a crypto-focused hedge fund.

Bitcoin was trading at around $82,500 on Friday, remaining above the miners' estimated costs. However, many miners can continue operations even if the price falls below the average cost. The market has room to decline toward the $74,300–$59,450 zone before miners experience significant financial strain, according to Charles Edwards, founder of Capriole Investments. Edwards highlighted the ongoing 'Bitcoin miner exodus' as a factor contributing to the bearish outlook. The hash rate for Bitcoin dropped to mid-2025 levels at the end of January, with some analysts suggesting that miners may have shifted resources to power AI operations, while others attributed the decline to the US winter storm.

Despite the hash rate dips, Bitcoin has historically rebounded, as noted by Jeff Feng, co-founder of Sei Labs. When miners shut down, the network adjusts by lowering mining difficulty over time, making it easier and cheaper for remaining miners to earn Bitcoin, thus stabilizing the network. Following China's 2021 mining ban, the hash rate fell by about 50%, causing Bitcoin's price to drop from approximately $64,000 to $29,000. However, the price recovered to $69,000 within five months. As of Friday, Bitcoin's fair price was estimated at around $120,950, based on its energy value—a metric that calculates Bitcoin's fair value using the network's energy and production inputs. Historically, Bitcoin tends to climb back toward its energy value after a prolonged downtrend, suggesting that the price could bottom anywhere from $74,300 to $59,450, with any rebound potentially triggering a mean-reversion move toward the energy value price.

data.title: New Jersey Governor Declares Energy Cost Emergency Amid Rising Electricity Prices
data.description:
New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill has declared an energy cost emergency in response to soaring electricity prices, according to Jin10. This move fulfills a campaign promise and serves as a warning to utility companies across the United States. The surge in electricity costs is attributed to the increasing demand on the power grid from artificial intelligence data centers.

Residents in New Jersey are experiencing higher electricity price hikes compared to most other regions. To address this, Sherrill has introduced a series of measures, including direct subsidies funded by the state government to offset the rising costs. Additionally, she has instructed regulatory bodies to pause or modify utility companies' investment plans and to review their business models.

With gubernatorial elections set to take place in several U.S. states in 2026, New Jersey's actions may inspire similar measures elsewhere. For utility investors focused on AI growth narratives, such interventions are emerging as significant political risks.

data.title: Balaji: The fiat currency crisis has begun; Bitcoin's core value lies in its resistance to censorship.
data.description: Balaji, former CTO of Coinbase and general partner of A16z, published an article on the X platform stating that a fiat currency crisis has begun, and Bitcoin's core value proposition lies in its resistance to censorship. Balaji argues that while gold has value in highly organized countries, it also faces the risk of being tracked and confiscated. In contrast, digital gold (and cryptocurrencies), represented by Bitcoin, possesses invisibility, internationalization, immediacy, and internet-native attributes, surpassing physical gold in terms of transportability, programmability, and verifiability. Balaji points out that as gold prices rise, the taxation or confiscation of physical gold will increase. He predicts that Eastern countries like China may revert to a gold standard, a digital gold standard, or a hybrid of both, while the internet will replace the US dollar with digital gold and cryptographically verifiable digital assets. Furthermore, Balaji advises investors to consider withdrawing from G7 countries and other regions highly dependent on the US dollar amid the deepening sovereign debt crisis, emphasizing that in the current environment, geographical location is more important than asset allocation.

data.title: The loan health ratio on Bhutan's sovereign wealth fund Aave has fallen to 1.15.
data.description: On-chain data shows that the loan health ratio of Druk Holdings' main wallet on Aave is currently 1.15. From January 19th to 20th, Druk Holdings' linked address leveraged long positions in ETH. As of press time, this address holds 116,560 stETH, representing a floating loss of approximately 15% from its purchase cost. All of this stETH is currently held in Aave, with the main wallet holding 104,000 stETH, currently valued at approximately $284 million, and its loan amount is 200 million USDT. In addition, the Kingdom of Bhutan's government address recently transferred 100.82 BTC, worth approximately $8.31 million, to QCP Capital's address.

data.description: According to GMGN data, MOLT, the Base Chain ecosystem token, saw its intraday price surge by over 460 times, pushing its market capitalization above $40 million. It is currently valued at approximately $38 million. Odaily reminds users that the token price is highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.description: The Czech Central Bank Governor has emphasized the importance of understanding Bitcoin rather than opposing future developments. According to Odaily, he also predicted that more countries are likely to adopt Bitcoin in the future.

data.title: Bitcoin-to-Gold Ratio Shows Signs of Recovery Amid Gold Rally
data.description: The bitcoin-to-gold ratio has recently rebounded from its previous lows, displaying patterns reminiscent of the 2019-2020 period. According to NS3.AI, this recovery aligns with a six-month rally in gold prices, indicating possible similarities in market behavior between bitcoin and gold. The correlation between these two assets suggests that investors might observe parallel trends in their performance, reflecting historical patterns seen in past years.

data.title: Gold Prices Recover Above $5100 Per Ounce After Early Decline
data.description: Spot gold prices have rebounded above the crucial $5100 per ounce level following an earlier drop. According to NS3.AI, the recovery saw an increase of nearly $160 from the day's low, mitigating the intraday decline to approximately 5%. This shift suggests a partial improvement in market sentiment towards gold during the trading session.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: Falling Dollar Liquidity Explains Recent Bitcoin Weakness
data.description:
Arthur Hayes links Bitcoin’s recent pullback to a sharp contraction in dollar liquidity caused by a surge in the US Treasury’s cash balance.

The post Arthur Hayes: Falling Dollar Liquidity Explains Recent Bitcoin Weakness first appeared on Crypto News And Market Updates | BTCUSA.

source: https://btcusa.com/arthur-hayes-falling-dollar-liquidity-explains-recent-bitcoin-weakness/
